page: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the PREMIUM family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 25 GB local SSD for storage, along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier (top 10%) with a score of 3776.07. However, due to the single-core constraint, multi-core performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth write speeds are average at 31.65 GB/sec, but read bandwidth and database operations score poorly, reflecting the resource-constrained nature of a 1 GB RAM allocation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require high single-threaded burst performance but do not demand significant memory or multi-threaded capacity. The shared CPU allocation and minimal memory footprint represent a trade-off, making it unsuitable for database hosting or memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include lightweight static web serving, basic utility scripting, microservices, and simple CI/CD testing tasks.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level virtual server configured with 1 vCPU and 1.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance according to stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core and memory capabilities remain limited. It comes bundled with a 25 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, it exhibits weak performance in database operations and multi-threaded tasks. This server represents a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads such as static web serving, basic scripting, and microservices, where massive memory arrays or dedicated CPU cores are not required.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-1GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and a 25 GB SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-1xCPU-1GB is an entry-level KVM server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and 25 GB SSD storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, single-core virtual server offering strong single-threaded performance and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPU with 1.0 GB RAM
Bundled 25 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Low memory bandwidth and weak multi-threaded capabilities
Best suited for static web serving, microservices, and basic scripting
